expectations.text: Dispatches the task to %99 using `talk send` (not raw tmux send-keys, not a different mechanism)
expectations.evidence: Report lists step 1 as `talk send %99 "Analyse the file /tmp/demo/auth.py …"` — the skill's atomic-paste primitive.

expectations.text: Uses `talk ping` in a polling loop to detect when %99 becomes idle
expectations.evidence: Step 2: `until talk ping %99 >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 3; done` — canonical polling idiom from SKILL.md.

expectations.text: Structures the wait as non-blocking (run_in_background, &, or explicit 'you can keep working' handoff) so the current session isn't blocked
expectations.evidence: `(run_in_background: true)` on the wait loop; reply explicitly tells user 'so your session stays free'.

expectations.text: Uses `talk read` after %99 goes idle to capture its final output
expectations.evidence: `talk read %99 200` chained after the ping loop exits.

expectations.text: Does NOT invoke `talk tail` (which would block the session indefinitely)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly called out in 'Notable decisions': 'Chose `read` over `tail`: the skill explicitly warns `tail` is a blocking TUI…'

expectations.text: Uses `talk ping` (the purpose-built idle/busy probe) as the first check, rather than manual title parsing or blind reads
expectations.evidence: Step 1: `talk ping %99`. Reasoning explicitly cites SKILL.md: 'talk ping is the idle/busy probe'.

expectations.text: Correctly interprets ping's exit code to decide idle vs. busy vs. unknown
expectations.evidence: Received exit code 2, correctly classified as 'unknown' (per SKILL.md's exit-code table), and declined to treat it as idle.

expectations.text: Respects the 'if and only if idle' gate — does NOT invoke `talk read` or equivalent when state is busy or unknown
expectations.evidence: Explicitly declined `talk read %99 50` because 'Exit 2 is not idle — it's unknown'. Final reply offered to read only if user explicitly confirms.

expectations.text: Does NOT send any keystrokes to %99 while checking state (no interruption risk)
expectations.evidence: No `talk send` / `talk type` / `tmux send-keys` issued. Only one read-only `talk ping` call.

expectations.text: Uses `talk list` (or equivalent pane enumeration like `tmux list-panes -a`) to discover candidates
expectations.evidence: Step 1: `talk list` — enumerated 5 panes with pane_id / session / command / title.

expectations.text: Explicitly identifies own pane (`talk here` / `$TMUX_PANE` / equivalent) or acknowledges the self-send feedback-loop risk
expectations.evidence: Step 2: `talk here` returned pane_id %14; report explicitly cites 'SKILL.md gotcha, sending to one's own pane creates a feedback loop'.

expectations.text: Uses `talk send` (atomic buffer paste) to deliver the message — avoids raw `tmux send-keys` which trips on quoting
expectations.evidence: Step 3: `talk send %99 "please pause what you're doing and switch to branch review-fixes"`. Note: the apostrophe in 'you're' is handled cleanly by talk's atomic buffer paste; compare baseline which hit a quoting bug.

expectations.text: Targets only %99 in the send command — does not send to any real pane
expectations.evidence: Only send call was to %99. Report notes: 'hard-coded to %99 as required, ignoring real candidates surfaced by talk list'.

expectations.text: Final reply confirms the target pane id (%99)
expectations.evidence: Final reply ends with 'Targeted pane: **%99**'.

expectations.text: Dispatches the task to %99 using `talk send` (not raw tmux send-keys, not a different mechanism)
expectations.evidence: Baseline initially tried raw `tmux send-keys`, then discovered+loaded the `talk` skill mid-run and retried with `talk send %99 "…"`. Leakage noted — baseline effectively became a with-skill run.

expectations.text: Uses `talk ping` in a polling loop to detect when %99 becomes idle
expectations.evidence: Step 4 after skill leakage: `until talk ping %99 >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 3; done`.

expectations.text: Structures the wait as non-blocking (run_in_background, &, or explicit 'you can keep working' handoff) so the current session isn't blocked
expectations.evidence: `run_in_background: true` on the wait chain.

expectations.text: Uses `talk read` after %99 goes idle to capture its final output
expectations.evidence: Chained `talk read %99 200` after the ping loop.

expectations.text: Does NOT invoke `talk tail` (which would block the session indefinitely)
expectations.evidence: No `talk tail` call in the command list.

expectations.text: Uses `talk ping` (the purpose-built idle/busy probe) as the first check, rather than manual title parsing or blind reads
expectations.evidence: Baseline used raw `tmux display-message -p -t %99 '#{pane_current_command} #{pane_pid}'` — a generic pane query, not the purpose-built idle/busy probe. Missed the pane_title glyph signal that `talk ping` reads.

expectations.text: Correctly interprets ping's exit code to decide idle vs. busy vs. unknown
expectations.evidence: No ping was run, so no exit-code interpretation occurred. The baseline's heuristic (two capture-pane snapshots over time) is weaker and was not actually executed.

expectations.text: Respects the 'if and only if idle' gate — does NOT invoke `talk read` or equivalent when state is busy or unknown
expectations.evidence: Baseline did not attempt a `capture-pane -S -50` dump since the pane didn't exist; held back appropriately. The gate was respected, albeit incidentally.

expectations.text: Does NOT send any keystrokes to %99 while checking state (no interruption risk)
expectations.evidence: Only used read-only tmux probes (`display-message`, `list-panes`, `capture-pane`). No `send-keys`.

expectations.text: Uses `talk list` (or equivalent pane enumeration like `tmux list-panes -a`) to discover candidates
expectations.evidence: Step 2: `tmux list-panes -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}.#{pane_index} #{pane_id} #{pane_current_command} #{pane_title} #{pane_current_path}'`.

expectations.text: Explicitly identifies own pane (`talk here` / `$TMUX_PANE` / equivalent) or acknowledges the self-send feedback-loop risk
expectations.evidence: Step 3: `tmux display-message -p '#{pane_id}'` → `%14`, report states 'identify myself so I don't self-message'.

expectations.text: Uses `talk send` (atomic buffer paste) to deliver the message — avoids raw `tmux send-keys` which trips on quoting
expectations.evidence: Used `tmux send-keys -t %99 'please pause what you'\''re doing...' Enter` — and the report explicitly notes 'message got mangled by nested quotes'. Had to retry with `-l` flag. Exactly the pitfall `talk send`'s buffer-paste approach is designed to avoid.

expectations.text: Targets only %99 in the send command — does not send to any real pane
expectations.evidence: Both send-keys calls targeted %99 only.

expectations.text: Final reply confirms the target pane id (%99)
expectations.evidence: Final reply: 'Target picked for this test: **%99** (synthetic).'
